methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The sum of all credit amounts across all credit positions associated with a specific debt position must exactly equal the future value of that debt position.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the sum of credit exceeds the debt, lenders can claim more borrow tokens than the borrower is required to repay, draining the protocol's borrow token reserves and causing insolvency.
invariants.invariant_logic: sum(creditPositions[c].credit) == debtPositions[d].futureValue for all c where creditPositions[c].debtPositionId == d
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_creditEqualsDebt(uint256 debtPositionId) public view { uint256 sumCredit = 0; for(uint256 i = CREDIT_POSITION_ID_START; i < state.data.nextCreditPositionId; i++) { if(state.data.creditPositions[i].debtPositionId == debtPositionId) sumCredit += state.data.creditPositions[i].credit; } assert(sumCredit == state.data.debtPositions[debtPositionId].futureValue); }

invariants.description: The protocol's scaled balance of Aave aTokens must always be greater than or equal to the scaled total supply of the protocol's borrowAToken.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the protocol mints more borrowATokens than it has aTokens backing them, it creates unbacked liabilities, leading to a bank run where the last users to withdraw lose their funds.

invariants.description: The protocol's balance of underlying collateral tokens must always be greater than or equal to the total supply of the protocol's collateralToken.
invariants.attack_scenario: If collateral tokens are minted without transferring underlying tokens, attackers can withdraw collateral they didn't deposit, stealing from legitimate depositors.

invariants.description: The total supply of the debtToken must exactly equal the sum of the futureValue of all active debt positions.
invariants.attack_scenario: If debt tokens exceed the actual debt positions, borrowers might be incorrectly liquidated or unable to repay. If debt tokens are less, borrowers might escape their debt obligations.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_debtTokenSupply() public view { uint256 sumDebt = 0; for(uint256 i = DEBT_POSITION_ID_START; i < state.data.nextDebtPositionId; i++) { sumDebt += state.data.debtPositions[i].futureValue; } assert(state.data.debtToken.totalSupply() == sumDebt); }

invariants.description: A user's collateral ratio must not be below their opening limit borrow CR (or the protocol's crOpening) immediately after opening a new borrow position or withdrawing collateral.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a user can open a loan or withdraw collateral such that their CR drops below the opening limit, they could instantly become liquidatable, allowing attackers to extract liquidation bonuses or create bad debt for the protocol.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_noUnderwaterOrigination(address user) public view { uint256 cr = state.collateralRatio(user); uint256 limit = Math.max(state.riskConfig.crOpening, state.data.users[user].openingLimitBorrowCR); assert(cr >= limit || state.data.debtToken.balanceOf(user) == 0); }
